description: Machine-oriented index for LLM agents. Paths are relative to docs/.

items.title: API: `addChat(id, role, value)`
items.name: addChat
items.summary: Appends a new message to the end of the current chat.

items.title: API: `alertConfirm(id, value)`
items.name: alertConfirm
items.summary: Asks the user a yes/no question and resolves to a boolean.

items.title: API: `alertError(id, value)`
items.name: alertError
items.summary: Shows an error alert to the user.

items.title: API: `alertInput(id, value)`
items.name: alertInput
items.summary: Prompts the user for a line of text and resolves to what they typed.

items.title: API: `alertNormal(id, value)`
items.name: alertNormal
items.summary: Shows an informational alert/toast to the user.

items.title: API: `alertSelect(id, value)`
items.name: alertSelect
items.summary: Presents the user with a list of choices and resolves to the one they pick.

items.title: API: `axLLM(id, prompt, useMultimodal, options)`
items.name: axLLM
items.summary: Runs a sub-request against the auxiliary model and returns the decoded result. Identical in shape to `LLM` but routes to the auxiliary model (`'otherAx'`) instead of the main one. This is the high-level wrapper over the raw `axLLMMain`: it JSON-encodes the prompt/options, awaits, and JSON-decodes the reply.

items.title: API: `axLLMMain(id, promptStr, useMultimodal, optionsStr)`
items.name: axLLMMain
items.summary: The raw auxiliary-model sub-request: JSON string in, JSON string out. Routes to the `'otherAx'` (auxiliary) model. Behaves exactly like `LLMMain` except for the target model. Prefer the `axLLM` preamble helper, which handles JSON for you.

items.title: API: `cbs(value)`
items.name: cbs
items.summary: Expands a CBS `{{...}}` template string in the current character context.

items.title: API: `cutChat(id, start, end)`
items.name: cutChat
items.summary: Trims the chat to a contiguous range of messages.

items.title: API: `generateImage(id, value, negValue)`
items.name: generateImage
items.summary: Generates an AI image from a prompt (and optional negative prompt), stores it in Risu's inlay store, and returns an `{{inlay::id}}` token you can drop into chat text or feed back into a multimodal LLM call.

items.title: API: `getAuthorsNote(id)`
items.name: getAuthorsNote
items.summary: Returns the current chat's author's note (`chat.note`).

items.title: API: `getBackgroundEmbedding(id)`
items.name: getBackgroundEmbedding
items.summary: Returns the selected character's background embedding HTML (`char.backgroundHTML`).

items.title: API: `getCharacterFirstMessage(id)`
items.name: getCharacterFirstMessage
items.summary: Returns the selected character's first/greeting message (`char.firstMessage`).

items.title: API: `getCharacterImage(id)`
items.name: getCharacterImage
items.summary: Returns the selected character's image as an `{{inlayed::id}}` token, or an empty string if there is no usable image. This is a thin preamble wrapper that just awaits the raw `getCharacterImageMain`.

items.title: API: `getCharacterImageMain(id)`
items.name: getCharacterImageMain
items.summary: The raw host call behind `getCharacterImage`. Loads the selected character's image into Risu's inlay store and returns an `{{inlayed::id}}` token, or `''`. The preamble helper `getCharacterImage` simply awaits this; prefer it unless you have a reason to call the raw form.

items.title: API: `getCharacterLastMessage(id)`
items.name: getCharacterLastMessage
items.summary: Returns the data of the most recent char-role message in the chat.

items.title: API: `getChat(id, index)`
items.name: getChat
items.summary: Returns one chat message as a decoded Lua table `{role, data, time}`.

items.title: API: `getChatLength(id)`
items.name: getChatLength
items.summary: Returns the number of messages currently in the chat.

items.title: API: `getChatMain(id, index)`
items.name: getChatMain
items.summary: Returns one chat message as a JSON string. This is the raw host call; in Lua prefer the `getChat` preamble helper, which decodes the JSON for you.
items.layer: Host API (`declareAPI`) â€” raw form of [`getChat`](getChat.md)

items.title: API: `getChatVar(id, key)`
items.name: getChatVar
items.summary: Reads the current value of a persistent chat variable as a string.

items.title: API: `getDescription(id)`
items.name: getDescription
items.summary: Returns the description (`desc`) of the currently selected character.

items.title: API: `getFullChat(id)`
items.name: getFullChat
items.summary: Returns the whole chat as a decoded Lua array of `{role, data, time}` tables.

items.title: API: `getFullChatMain(id)`
items.name: getFullChatMain
items.summary: Returns the whole chat as a JSON string: an array of message objects. This is the raw host call; in Lua prefer the `getFullChat` preamble helper, which decodes the JSON for you.
items.layer: Host API (`declareAPI`) â€” raw form of [`getFullChat`](getFullChat.md)

items.title: API: `getGlobalVar(id, key)`
items.name: getGlobalVar
items.summary: Reads a global chat variable shared across all chats.

items.title: API: `getLoreBooks(id, search)`
items.name: getLoreBooks
items.summary: Finds lorebook entries whose `comment` exactly matches `search`, across all three lore scopes, and returns them as a Lua array. This is the preamble wrapper over `getLoreBooksMain`: it calls the raw function and `json.decode`s the result for you.

items.title: API: `getLoreBooksMain(id, search)`
items.name: getLoreBooksMain
items.summary: The raw host call behind `getLoreBooks`: same exact `comment` lookup across the three lore scopes, but returns a JSON string rather than a decoded table. Prefer the `getLoreBooks` preamble helper, which decodes for you.

items.title: API: `getName(id)`
items.name: getName
items.summary: Returns the name of the currently selected character.

items.title: API: `getPersonaDescription(id)`
items.name: getPersonaDescription
items.summary: Returns the active persona prompt, CBS-parsed in the selected-character context.

items.title: API: `getPersonaImage(id)`
items.name: getPersonaImage
items.summary: Returns the current persona (user) icon as an `{{inlayed::id}}` token, or an empty string if there is no usable icon. A thin preamble wrapper that awaits the raw `getPersonaImageMain`.

items.title: API: `getPersonaImageMain(id)`
items.name: getPersonaImageMain
items.summary: The raw host call behind `getPersonaImage`. Loads the current persona (user) icon into Risu's inlay store and returns an `{{inlayed::id}}` token, or `''`. The preamble helper `getPersonaImage` simply awaits this; prefer it unless you have a reason to call the raw form.

items.title: API: `getPersonaName(id)`
items.name: getPersonaName
items.summary: Returns the active user/persona name (`getUserName()`).

items.title: API: `getState(id, name)`
items.name: getState
items.summary: Reads a JSON-decoded "state" value stored in a `__`-prefixed chat variable.

items.title: API: `getTokens(id, value)`
items.name: getTokens
items.summary: Returns the token count of a string using Risu's active tokenizer.

items.title: API: `getUserLastMessage(id)`
items.name: getUserLastMessage
items.summary: Returns the data of the most recent user-role message in the chat.

items.title: API: `hash(id, value)`
items.name: hash
items.summary: Hashes a string using Risu's `hasher`.

items.title: API: `insertChat(id, index, role, value)`
items.name: insertChat
items.summary: Inserts a new message at a given index, shifting later messages down.

items.title: API: `listenEdit(type, func)`
items.name: listenEdit
items.summary: Registers a chained edit-trigger handler for one of the four edit hooks.
items.permission_tier: N/A (registration only â€” the registered handler runs under the edit hook's key)
items.async: no (registration is synchronous; the handler may be `async`)

items.title: API: `LLM(id, prompt, useMultimodal, options)`
items.name: LLM
items.summary: Runs a sub-request against the chat's main model and returns the decoded result. This is the high-level convenience wrapper over the raw `LLMMain`: it JSON-encodes the prompt and options for you, awaits the host call, and JSON-decodes the `{ success, result }` reply back into a Lua table.

items.title: API: `LLMMain(id, promptStr, useMultimodal, optionsStr)`
items.name: LLMMain
items.summary: The raw main-model sub-request: JSON string in, JSON string out. Routes to the `'model'` (main) model. Prefer the `LLM` preamble helper, which JSON-encodes the prompt/options and JSON-decodes the reply for you. Call this directly only if you need to control the JSON yourself.

items.title: API: `loadLoreBooks(id)`
items.name: loadLoreBooks
items.summary: Runs Risu's real activated-lore selection and returns the prompt-ready entries, decoded into a Lua array. Unlike `getLoreBooks` (an exact `comment` lookup), this performs the actual activation Risu would use when building a request. It is the preamble wrapper over the raw `loadLoreBooksMain`: it awaits the host call and `json.decode`s the result. The helper passes no reserve token budget.

items.title: API: `loadLoreBooksMain(id, reserve)`
items.name: loadLoreBooksMain
items.summary: The raw activated-lore loader behind `loadLoreBooks`. Runs Risu's real lore activation and returns a JSON array of prompt-ready entries, honoring a caller-supplied `reserve` token budget. Prefer the `loadLoreBooks` preamble helper, which awaits and decodes for you (but passes no reserve).

items.title: API: `log(value)`
items.name: log
items.summary: Logs any Lua value to the developer console by JSON-encoding it.

items.title: API: `logMain(value)`
items.name: logMain
items.summary: Raw console logger: prints a parsed JSON string to the developer console.

items.title: API: `reloadChat(id, index)`
items.name: reloadChat
items.summary: Requests a re-render of a single chat message.

items.title: API: `reloadDisplay(id)`
items.name: reloadDisplay
items.summary: Requests a refresh of the whole chat display / GUI.

items.title: API: `removeChat(id, index)`
items.name: removeChat
items.summary: Removes a single message from the chat.

items.title: API: `request(id, url)`
items.name: request
items.summary: Performs an HTTPS GET request and returns the response as a JSON string. Heavily restricted: HTTPS only, short URLs only, rate-limited, and a few hosts are blocked outright.

items.title: API: `setBackgroundEmbedding(id, data)`
items.name: setBackgroundEmbedding
items.summary: Sets the selected character's background embedding HTML (`char.backgroundHTML`).

items.title: API: `setCharacterFirstMessage(id, data)`
items.name: setCharacterFirstMessage
items.summary: Sets the selected character's first/greeting message (`char.firstMessage`).

items.title: API: `setChat(id, index, value)`
items.name: setChat
items.summary: Replaces the text of the message at a given index.

items.title: API: `setChatRole(id, index, value)`
items.name: setChatRole
items.summary: Changes the role of the message at a given index.

items.title: API: `setChatVar(id, key, value)`
items.name: setChatVar
items.summary: Writes a persistent chat variable.

items.title: API: `setDescription(id, desc)`
items.name: setDescription
items.summary: Sets the description (`desc`) of the currently selected character.

items.title: API: `setFullChat(id, value)`
items.name: setFullChat
items.summary: Replaces the entire chat from a Lua array of `{role, data}` tables.

items.title: API: `setFullChatMain(id, value)`
items.name: setFullChatMain
items.summary: Replaces the entire chat message array from a JSON string. This is the raw host call; in Lua prefer the `setFullChat` preamble helper, which JSON-encodes for you.
items.layer: Host API (`declareAPI`) â€” raw form of [`setFullChat`](setFullChat.md)

items.title: API: `setName(id, name)`
items.name: setName
items.summary: Sets the name of the currently selected character.

items.title: API: `setState(id, name, value)`
items.name: setState
items.summary: Writes a JSON-encoded "state" value into a `__`-prefixed chat variable.

items.title: API: `similarity(id, source, value)`
items.name: similarity
items.summary: Embedding-based similarity search. The candidate strings in `value` are embedded with the configured embedding model (via `HypaProcesser`), then ranked against the embedded `source` query. Returns the candidates sorted by similarity to `source`.

items.title: API: `simpleLLM(id, prompt)`
items.name: simpleLLM
items.summary: A one-shot main-model call: pass a single user-message string, get back the model's reply. Unlike `LLM`/`LLMMain`, there is no role array and no JSON encoding â€” `prompt` becomes one `{ role = 'user', content = prompt }` message. There is no preamble helper; this host call returns a Lua table directly (it does not return a JSON string).

items.title: API: `sleep(id, time)`
items.name: sleep
items.summary: Pauses the handler for a given number of milliseconds.

items.title: API: `stopChat(id)`
items.name: stopChat
items.summary: Halts the current send by setting the run's `stopSending` flag.

items.title: API: `upsertLocalLoreBook(id, name, content, options)`
items.name: upsertLocalLoreBook
items.summary: Creates or replaces a current-chat local lorebook entry, keyed by its `comment` (`name`). Any existing chat-local entry with the same `comment` is removed first, then a fresh entry is pushed. Only chat-local lore is writable from Lua â€” there is no API to edit character-global or module lore.

items.title: CBS: `{{average::...}}`
items.name: average
items.summary: Returns the arithmetic mean of a set of numbers.

items.title: CBS: `{{max::...}}`
items.name: max
items.summary: Returns the largest of a set of numbers.

items.title: CBS: `{{min::...}}`
items.name: min
items.summary: Returns the smallest of a set of numbers.

items.title: CBS: `{{sum::...}}`
items.name: sum
items.summary: Adds up a set of numbers.

items.title: CBS: `{{arrayassert::array::index::value}}`
items.name: arrayassert
items.summary: Ensures a given index exists in a JSON array, extending it if necessary.

items.title: CBS: `{{arrayelement::array::index}}`
items.name: arrayelement
items.summary: Returns the element at a given index of a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{arraylength::array}}`
items.name: arraylength
items.summary: Returns the number of elements in a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{arraypop::array}}`
items.name: arraypop
items.summary: Returns a JSON array with its last element removed.

items.title: CBS: `{{arraypush::array::element}}`
items.name: arraypush
items.summary: Returns a JSON array with one element appended to the end.

items.title: CBS: `{{arrayshift::array}}`
items.name: arrayshift
items.summary: Returns a JSON array with its first element removed.

items.title: CBS: `{{arraysplice::array::index::deleteCount::element}}`
items.name: arraysplice
items.summary: Removes and/or inserts elements at an index and returns the modified array.

items.title: CBS: `{{join::array::sep}}`
items.name: join
items.summary: Joins the elements of a JSON array into one string with a separator.

items.title: CBS: `{{makearray::a::b::...}}`
items.name: makearray
items.summary: Builds a JSON array from the supplied arguments.

items.title: CBS: `{{range::spec}}`
items.name: range
items.summary: Generates a JSON array of a numeric sequence.

items.title: CBS: `{{spread::array}}`
items.name: spread
items.summary: Joins a JSON array with `::` so it can be spread into another CBS call's arguments.

items.title: CBS: `{{asset::name}}`
items.name: asset
items.summary: Renders a character/module asset as an image or a video, chosen by file extension.

items.title: CBS: `{{assetlist}}`
items.name: assetlist
items.summary: Returns a JSON array of the current character's additional asset names.

items.title: CBS: `{{audio::name}}`
items.name: audio
items.summary: Renders an audio player for a named asset.

items.title: CBS: `{{bg::name}}`
items.name: bg
items.summary: Renders a full-size background div from a named asset â€” only in background mode.

items.title: CBS: `{{bgm::name}}`
items.name: bgm
items.summary: Emits a hidden background-music control element.

items.title: CBS: `{{button::text::trigger}}`
items.name: button
items.summary: Emits an HTML button that runs a manual trigger when clicked.

items.title: CBS: `{{chardisplayasset}}`
items.name: chardisplayasset
items.summary: Returns a JSON array of the character's display asset names, filtered by the prebuilt-asset exclusion settings.

items.title: CBS: `{{emotion::name}}`
items.name: emotion
items.summary: Renders an emotion image for the current character.

items.title: CBS: `{{emotionlist}}`
items.name: emotionlist
items.summary: Returns a JSON array of the current character's emotion image names.

items.title: CBS: `{{file::name::base64}}`
items.name: file
items.summary: Renders a filename chip in display mode, or decodes embedded base64 content otherwise.

items.title: CBS: `{{image::name}}`
items.name: image
items.summary: Renders a framed image block.

items.title: CBS: `{{img::name}}`
items.name: img
items.summary: Renders a bare inline image.

items.title: CBS: `{{inlay::id}}`
items.name: inlay
items.summary: Renders an inlay-store asset by id, unstyled. Display only.

items.title: CBS: `{{inlayed::id}}`
items.name: inlayed
items.summary: Renders an inlay-store asset by id, styled. Display only.

items.title: CBS: `{{inlayeddata::id}}`
items.name: inlayeddata
items.summary: Renders an inlay-store asset by id, styled â€” and is included in the model request.

items.title: CBS: `{{moduleassetlist::namespace}}`
items.name: moduleassetlist
items.summary: Returns a JSON array of a module's asset names.

items.title: CBS: `{{path::name}}`
items.name: path
items.summary: Expands to the bare URL/path of a named asset (no HTML tag).

items.title: CBS: `{{raw::name}}`
items.name: raw
items.summary: Expands to the bare URL/path of a named asset (no HTML tag).

items.title: CBS: `{{risu::size}}`
items.name: risu
items.summary: Emits the Risu logo as an ``.

items.title: CBS: `{{source::char}}` / `{{source::user}}`
items.name: source
items.summary: Expands to the character or user profile image URL/path (bare, no HTML tag).

items.title: CBS: `{{video-img::name}}`
items.name: video-img
items.summary: Renders a muted, looping video â€” background-style, no controls.

items.title: CBS: `{{video::name}}`
items.name: video
items.summary: Renders a video player with controls.

items.title: CBS: `{{? expression}}`
items.summary: Inline calculator: evaluates a math expression and returns the result.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`?`, doc-only entry); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`matcher`, the `p1.startsWith('? ')` branch) via `calcString` in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/process/infunctions.ts`

items.title: CBS: `{{// comment}}`
items.summary: A hidden comment whose content is removed entirely.

items.title: CBS: `{{#each ARRAY as V}} ... {{/each}}`
items.name: #each
items.summary: Iterates over a JSON array, rendering the body once per element. The current element is read with `{{slot::V}}`.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#each`, doc-only); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher` returning the `each` type, and the `each` handling in the block-end loop)

items.title: CBS: `{{:else}}`
items.name: {{:else}}
items.summary: The else clause inside a `{{#when}}` block.

items.title: CBS: `{{#escape}} ... {{/escape}}`
items.name: #escape
items.summary: Escapes braces and parentheses in its body, treating the content as literal text.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#escape`, doc-only); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher` returning the `escape` type, and the `escape` case in `blockEndMatcher` calling `risuEscape`)

items.title: CBS: `{{#if_pure cond}} ... {{/if_pure}}`
items.name: #if_pure
items.summary: Legacy conditional that preserves whitespace. Deprecated â€” use `{{#when::keep::cond}}` instead.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#if_pure`, doc-only, marked `deprecated`); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher`, the `p1.startsWith('#if')` branch returning the `ifpure` type)

items.title: CBS: `{{#if cond}} ... {{/if}}`
items.name: #if
items.summary: Legacy conditional block. Deprecated â€” use `{{#when}}` instead.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#if`, doc-only, marked `deprecated`); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher`, the `p1.startsWith('#if')` branch)

items.title: CBS: `{{position::name}}`
items.name: position
items.summary: Declares a named position that lorebook `@@position` injection can target.

items.title: CBS: `{{#pure}} ... {{/pure}}`
items.name: #pure
items.summary: Legacy raw block: renders its contents without CBS processing. Deprecated â€” use `{{#puredisplay}}` instead.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#pure`, doc-only, marked `deprecated`); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher` returning the `pure` type)

items.title: CBS: `{{#puredisplay}} ... {{/puredisplay}}`
items.name: #puredisplay
items.summary: Renders content without CBS processing, escaping the braces so a later pass cannot re-parse them.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#puredisplay`, doc-only); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher` returning the `pure-display` type, and the `pure-display` handling in the parse loop)

items.title: CBS: `{{slot::name}}`
items.name: slot
items.summary: The current loop/block slot value, used inside `{{#each}}`.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`slot`, doc-only); substituted in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (the `each` block handling, which `replaceAll`s `{{slot::<name>}}`)

items.title: CBS: `{{#when ...}} ... {{/when}}`
items.name: #when
items.summary: The current conditional block: renders its body only when the condition is truthy. Replaces the deprecated `{{#if}}`.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/cbs.ts` (`#when`, doc-only); evaluated in `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/parser/parser.svelte.ts` (`blockStartMatcher`, the `p1.startsWith('#when')` branch, and `blockEndMatcher` for the `newif` / `newif-falsy` types)

items.title: CBS: `{{blank}}`
items.name: blank
items.summary: Returns an empty string.

items.title: CBS: `{{chatindex}}`
items.name: chatindex
items.summary: Returns the index of the message currently being processed.

items.title: CBS: `{{firstmsgindex}}`
items.name: firstmsgindex
items.summary: Returns the index of the selected first message / alternate greeting.

items.title: CBS: `{{isfirstmsg}}`
items.name: isfirstmsg
items.summary: Returns whether the template is being expanded in a first-message context.

items.title: CBS: `{{jbtoggled}}`
items.name: jbtoggled
items.summary: Returns whether the jailbreak prompt is enabled.

items.title: CBS: `{{maxcontext}}`
items.name: maxcontext
items.summary: Returns the configured maximum context length.

items.title: CBS: `{{role}}`
items.name: role
items.summary: Returns the role of the current message.

items.title: CBS: `{{date::format::timestamp}}`
items.name: date
items.summary: Formats a date/time with a custom format string.

items.title: CBS: `{{idleduration}}`
items.name: idleduration
items.summary: Returns the time elapsed since the last message in the chat (HH:MM:SS).

items.title: CBS: `{{isodate}}`
items.name: isodate
items.summary: Returns the current UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD).

items.title: CBS: `{{isotime}}`
items.name: isotime
items.summary: Returns the current UTC time (HH:MM:SS).

items.title: CBS: `{{messagedate}}`
items.name: messagedate
items.summary: Returns the local date on which the current message was sent.

items.title: CBS: `{{messageidleduration}}`
items.name: messageidleduration
items.summary: Returns the elapsed time between the current user message and the previous user message (HH:MM:SS).

items.title: CBS: `{{messagetime}}`
items.name: messagetime
items.summary: Returns the local time at which the current message was sent (HH:MM:SS).

items.title: CBS: `{{messageunixtimearray}}`
items.name: messageunixtimearray
items.summary: Returns every message timestamp in the current chat as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{time}}`
items.name: time
items.summary: Returns the current local time.

items.title: CBS: `{{unixtime}}`
items.name: unixtime
items.summary: Returns the current unix time in seconds.

items.title: CBS: `{{dictelement::obj::key}}`
items.name: dictelement
items.summary: Reads a single value out of a JSON object by key.

items.title: CBS: `{{element::json::key1::key2::...}}`
items.name: element
items.summary: Walks a path of keys/indices into a nested JSON value and returns what it finds.

items.title: CBS: `{{filter::array::type}}`
items.name: filter
items.summary: Filters a JSON array, removing empty and/or duplicate entries.

items.title: CBS: `{{makedict::k=v::k2=v2::...}}`
items.name: makedict
items.summary: Builds a JSON object from `key=value` arguments.

items.title: CBS: `{{objectassert::obj::key::value}}`
items.name: objectassert
items.summary: Sets a default on a JSON object: writes the key only if it is not already present, then returns the object.

items.title: CBS: `{{crypt::s::shift}}`
items.name: crypt
items.summary: A Caesar-style shift cipher over UTF-16 code units; with the default shift it is its own inverse.

items.title: CBS: `{{fromhex::hex}}`
items.name: fromhex
items.summary: Converts a hexadecimal string to its decimal value.

items.title: CBS: `{{tohex::num}}`
items.name: tohex
items.summary: Converts a decimal number to its hexadecimal string.

items.title: CBS: `{{u::hex}}`
items.name: u
items.summary: Returns the character for a hexadecimal code unit.

items.title: CBS: `{{ue::hex}}`
items.name: ue
items.summary: Returns the character for a hexadecimal code unit. Despite the "encode" name, its behavior is identical to `{{u}}` (a *decode*).

items.title: CBS: `{{unicodedecode::codepoint}}`
items.name: unicodedecode
items.summary: Returns the character for a decimal code unit.

items.title: CBS: `{{unicodeencode::s::index}}`
items.name: unicodeencode
items.summary: Returns the UTF-16 code unit of a character in a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{xor::s}}`
items.name: xor
items.summary: Obfuscates a string by XOR-ing each byte with `0xFF` and base64-encoding the result.

items.title: CBS: `{{xordecrypt::b64}}`
items.name: xordecrypt
items.summary: Reverses `{{xor}}`: base64-decodes, then XORs each byte with `0xFF`.

items.title: CBS: `{{bkspc}}`
items.name: bkspc
items.summary: Removes the last word from the output produced so far.

items.title: CBS: `{{br}}`
items.name: br
items.summary: Emits a single literal newline character.

items.title: CBS: `{{cbr::n}}`
items.name: cbr
items.summary: Emits the escaped two-character sequence `\n`, optionally repeated.

items.title: CBS: `{{codeblock::code}}`
items.name: codeblock
items.summary: Renders text as a fenced code block, optionally with a language for syntax highlighting.

items.title: CBS: `{{comment::text}}`
items.name: comment
items.summary: Renders a visible, styled comment block.

items.title: CBS: `{{declare::name}}`
items.name: declare
items.summary: Sets a parser flag that other CBS behavior can read.

items.title: CBS: `{{erase}}`
items.name: erase
items.summary: Removes the last sentence from the output produced so far.

items.title: CBS: escaped literal characters
items.name: escaped literal characters
items.summary: A family of zero-argument CBS functions that emit characters which *display* as `{`, `}`, `{{`, `}}`, `(`, `)`, `<`, `>`, `:`, or `;` but are not re-parsed as CBS / HTML syntax.

items.title: CBS: `{{hiddenkey::keys}}`
items.name: hiddenkey
items.summary: Marks lorebook activation keys that never reach the model.

items.title: CBS: `{{iserror::s}}`
items.name: iserror
items.summary: Tests whether a string looks like an error message.

items.title: CBS: `{{ruby::text::ruby}}`
items.name: ruby
items.summary: Renders ruby text (furigana) as HTML.

items.title: CBS: `{{tex::latex}}`
items.name: tex
items.summary: Wraps an expression as display-mode LaTeX/KaTeX math.

items.title: CBS: `{{charhistory}}`
items.name: charhistory
items.summary: Returns all character messages as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{history}}` / `{{history::role}}`
items.name: history
items.summary: Returns the chat history as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{lastmessage}}`
items.name: lastmessage
items.summary: Returns the content of the last message in the chat, regardless of role.

items.title: CBS: `{{lastmessageid}}`
items.name: lastmessageid
items.summary: Returns the 0-based index of the last message in the chat.

items.title: CBS: `{{lorebook}}`
items.name: lorebook
items.summary: Returns all active lorebook entries as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{previouscharchat}}`
items.name: previouscharchat
items.summary: Returns the most recent character message, falling back to the greeting.

items.title: CBS: `{{previouschatlog::index}}`
items.name: previouschatlog
items.summary: Returns the content of the message at a given chat index.

items.title: CBS: `{{previoususerchat}}`
items.name: previoususerchat
items.summary: Returns the most recent user message.

items.title: CBS: `{{userhistory}}`
items.name: userhistory
items.summary: Returns all user messages as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{char}}`
items.name: char
items.summary: Returns the display name (or nickname) of the current character/bot.

items.title: CBS: `{{description}}`
items.name: description
items.summary: Returns the current character's description field.

items.title: CBS: `{{exampledialogue}}`
items.name: exampledialogue
items.summary: Returns the current character's example dialogue/messages.

items.title: CBS: `{{personality}}`
items.name: personality
items.summary: Returns the current character's personality field.

items.title: CBS: `{{scenario}}`
items.name: scenario
items.summary: Returns the current character's scenario field.

items.title: CBS: `{{trigger_id}}`
items.name: trigger_id
items.summary: Returns the `risu-id` of the element that fired the current manual trigger.

items.title: CBS: `{{user}}`
items.name: user
items.summary: Returns the active user/persona name.

items.title: CBS: `{{all::...}}`
items.name: all
items.summary: Logical AND across many values.

items.title: CBS: `{{and::a::b}}`
items.name: and
items.summary: Logical AND of two boolean values.

items.title: CBS: `{{any::...}}`
items.name: any
items.summary: Logical OR across many values.

items.title: CBS: `{{equal::a::b}}`
items.name: equal
items.summary: Tests two values for exact string equality.

items.title: CBS: `{{greater::a::b}}`
items.name: greater
items.summary: Numeric "greater than" comparison.

items.title: CBS: `{{greaterequal::a::b}}`
items.name: greaterequal
items.summary: Numeric "greater than or equal to" comparison.

items.title: CBS: `{{less::a::b}}`
items.name: less
items.summary: Numeric "less than" comparison.

items.title: CBS: `{{lessequal::a::b}}`
items.name: lessequal
items.summary: Numeric "less than or equal to" comparison.

items.title: CBS: `{{not::a}}`
items.name: not
items.summary: Logical NOT of a boolean value.

items.title: CBS: `{{notequal::a::b}}`
items.name: notequal
items.summary: Tests two values for string inequality.

items.title: CBS: `{{or::a::b}}`
items.name: or
items.summary: Logical OR of two boolean values.

items.title: CBS: `{{abs::n}}`
items.name: abs
items.summary: Returns the absolute value of a number.

items.title: CBS: `{{calc::expr}}`
items.name: calc
items.summary: Evaluates a math expression and returns the result.

items.title: CBS: `{{ceil::n}}`
items.name: ceil
items.summary: Rounds a number up to the nearest integer.

items.title: CBS: `{{fixnum::n::decimals}}`
items.name: fixnum
items.summary: Formats a number to a fixed number of decimal places.

items.title: CBS: `{{floor::n}}`
items.name: floor
items.summary: Rounds a number down to the nearest integer.

items.title: CBS: `{{pow::base::exp}}`
items.name: pow
items.summary: Raises a number to a power.

items.title: CBS: `{{remaind::a::b}}`
items.name: remaind
items.summary: Returns the remainder of dividing one number by another (modulo).

items.title: CBS: `{{round::n}}`
items.name: round
items.summary: Rounds a number to the nearest integer.

items.title: CBS: `{{axmodel}}`
items.name: axmodel
items.summary: Returns the id of the auxiliary / sub model.

items.title: CBS: `{{metadata::key}}`
items.name: metadata
items.summary: Returns a piece of system / application metadata selected by `key`.

items.title: CBS: `{{model}}`
items.name: model
items.summary: Returns the id of the currently selected AI model.

items.title: CBS: `{{prefillsupported}}`
items.name: prefillsupported
items.summary: Returns whether the current model supports response prefill.

items.title: CBS: `{{moduleenabled::namespace}}`
items.name: moduleenabled
items.summary: Tests whether a module is currently enabled.

items.title: CBS: `{{authornote}}`
items.name: authornote
items.summary: Returns the chat's author's note.

items.title: CBS: `{{globalnote}}`
items.name: globalnote
items.summary: Returns the global note (also called the system note).

items.title: CBS: `{{jb}}`
items.name: jb
items.summary: Returns the jailbreak prompt text.

items.title: CBS: `{{mainprompt}}`
items.name: mainprompt
items.summary: Returns the main system prompt.

items.title: CBS: `{{persona}}`
items.name: persona
items.summary: Returns the user persona prompt text.

items.title: CBS: `{{dice::XdY}}`
items.name: dice
items.summary: Rolls dice in `XdY` notation and returns the sum.

items.title: CBS: `{{hash::s}}`
items.name: hash
items.summary: Turns a string into a deterministic ~7-digit number.

items.title: CBS: `{{pick::...}}`
items.name: pick
items.summary: Like `{{random}}`, but the choice is hash-stable: it stays the same across re-renders of the same message.

items.title: CBS: `{{randint::min::max}}`
items.name: randint
items.summary: Returns a random integer in an inclusive range.

items.title: CBS: `{{random::...}}`
items.name: random
items.summary: Returns a random number, or a random element chosen from its arguments.

items.title: CBS: `{{roll::XdY}}`
items.name: roll
items.summary: Rolls dice (true random) with sensible defaults and returns the sum.

items.title: CBS: `{{rollp::XdY}}`
items.name: rollp
items.summary: Like `{{roll}}`, but hash-stable: the same message rolls the same result across re-renders.

items.title: CBS: `{{capitalize::s}}`
items.name: capitalize
items.summary: Capitalizes the first character of a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{contains::s::sub}}`
items.name: contains
items.summary: Tests whether a substring appears anywhere within a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{endswith::s::sub}}`
items.name: endswith
items.summary: Tests whether a string ends with a given substring.

items.title: CBS: `{{length::s}}`
items.name: length
items.summary: Returns the character count of a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{lower::s}}`
items.name: lower
items.summary: Converts a string to lowercase.

items.title: CBS: `{{replace::s::find::to}}`
items.name: replace
items.summary: Replaces every occurrence of a substring with another string.

items.title: CBS: `{{reverse::s}}`
items.name: reverse
items.summary: Reverses the input string.

items.title: CBS: `{{split::s::delim}}`
items.name: split
items.summary: Splits a string on a delimiter and returns the parts as a JSON array.

items.title: CBS: `{{startswith::s::sub}}`
items.name: startswith
items.summary: Tests whether a string begins with a given substring.

items.title: CBS: `{{tonumber::s}}`
items.name: tonumber
items.summary: Strips everything except digits and decimal points from a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{trim::s}}`
items.name: trim
items.summary: Strips leading and trailing whitespace from a string.

items.title: CBS: `{{upper::s}}`
items.name: upper
items.summary: Converts a string to uppercase.

items.title: CBS: `{{addvar::name::amount}}`
items.name: addvar
items.summary: Adds a number to a persistent chat variable.

items.title: CBS: `{{getglobalvar::name}}`
items.name: getglobalvar
items.summary: Reads a global chat variable, shared across all chats and characters.

items.title: CBS: `{{getvar::name}}`
items.name: getvar
items.summary: Returns the value of a persistent chat variable.

items.title: CBS: `{{return::value}}`
items.name: return
items.summary: Sets the parser's return value and forces it to stop.

items.title: CBS: `{{setdefaultvar::name::value}}`
items.name: setdefaultvar
items.summary: Sets a chat variable only if it is not already set.

items.title: CBS: `{{settempvar::name::value}}`
items.name: settempvar
items.summary: Sets a temporary variable that lives only during the current parse.

items.title: CBS: `{{setvar::name::value}}`
items.name: setvar
items.summary: Sets a persistent chat variable.

items.title: CBS: `{{tempvar::name}}`
items.name: tempvar
items.summary: Reads a temporary variable that lives only during the current parse.

items.title: Element: Access key (`id`) and permission tiers
items.name: id
items.summary: Lua never receives a raw handle to Risu's database. Instead Risu generates a fresh opaque key (a UUID) for each script run, passes it to your handler as the first argument (conventionally named `id`), and registers that key in one or more permission sets for the duration of the run. Every host call takes the key as its first argument and checks it against those sets before doing anything privileged.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/process/scriptings.ts` (`ScriptingSafeIds`, `ScriptingEditDisplayIds`, `ScriptingLowLevelIds`, and the per-mode key setup near the bottom of `runScripted`)

items.title: Element: Asset display tokens (`{{img::}}`, `{{video::}}`, â€¦)
items.name: img
items.summary: Tokens the display parser expands into image/video/audio/background HTML when it renders chat text and background embedding. These are display helpers, not multimodal attachments â€” contrast inlay tokens.

items.title: Element: Background embedding (`backgroundHTML`)
items.name: backgroundHTML
items.summary: Persistent HTML rendered behind the chat. Risu's UI calls it "background embedding", but the character field Lua touches is `backgroundHTML`.

items.title: Element: Chat message (`{role, data, time}`)
items.name: {role, data, time}
items.summary: The shape Lua sees for a stored chat message, and how it differs from the OpenAI-style request message used by LLM prompts and `editRequest`.

items.title: Element: Chat variables (per-chat string state)
items.name: Chat variables (per-chat string state)
items.summary: Persistent per-chat string variables, stored on the current chat and read with a defined fallback chain.

items.title: Element: Display HTML (sanitization & chat buttons)
items.name: Display HTML (sanitization & chat buttons)
items.summary: How Risu sanitizes the HTML it renders for chat / display output, and the custom attributes that turn markup into clickable triggers.

items.title: Element: Global variables (read-only from Lua)
items.name: Global variables (read-only from Lua)
items.summary: Cross-chat string variables stored in the database, readable from Lua but not writable.

items.title: Element: Inlay tokens (`{{inlay::}}`, `{{inlayed::}}`, `{{inlayeddata::}}`)
items.name: inlay
items.summary: Tokens that reference files in Risu's inlay storage. They render as media for display and can be attached as multimodal input to LLM calls â€” unlike asset display tokens.

items.title: Element: Lorebook entry
items.name: Lorebook entry
items.summary: The structured world-info entry Risu activates into prompts, and how Lua reads and writes it.

items.title: Element: Modules
items.name: Modules
items.summary: Reusable bundles of lore, regex, triggers, assets, toggles, MCP URLs, and background HTML. Lua has no API to list them, but enabled modules affect Lua indirectly.

items.title: Element: Promise / `async()` (awaiting host calls)
items.name: async
items.summary: Some host calls are asynchronous. Risu's Lua VM injects a `Promise` type and an `async()` wrapper so you can await them.
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/process/scriptings.ts` (`luaCodeWrapper` â€” the injected `async`/`Promise` plumbing and the high-level wrappers; the `async`/`await` host declarations)

items.title: Element: Prompt toggles (`toggle_` globals)
items.name: toggle_
items.summary: User-facing sidebar controls whose state is stored as `toggle_`-prefixed global variables, readable from Lua.

items.title: Element: Regex Script
items.name: Regex Script
items.summary: User-defined find/replace rules that run in Risu's text pipeline. Lua cooperates with them by writing marker text; Lua itself has no `editprocess` listener.

items.title: Hook: Custom / manual-trigger modes
items.name: Custom / manual-trigger modes
items.summary: Any trigger name that is not a built-in mode is dispatched to a global function of that exact name.
items.layer: Hook (custom mode â€” default global dispatch)
items.permission_tier: Safe (plus low-level if the trigger has `lowLevelAccess`)
items.fires: when a manual trigger / named button runs the pack's Lua with a mode that is not a built-in
items.source: `Refer/Risuai/src/ts/process/scriptings.ts` (the `default` case in the mode dispatch of `runScripted`; `runLuaButtonTrigger`)

items.title: Hook: `editDisplay` (mode `editDisplay`)
items.name: editDisplay
items.summary: `editDisplay` transforms what the user sees without changing the stored message.
items.permission_tier: Restricted edit-display â€” chat-var writes only; no chat/character mutation, no reload; never low-level
items.define: `listenEdit('editDisplay', function(id, value, meta) ... return value end)`
items.fires: during display rendering, on the text about to be shown (stored chat is unchanged)

items.title: Hook: `editInput` (mode `editInput`)
items.name: editInput
items.summary: `editInput` transforms the text the user just submitted before Risu stores it.
items.permission_tier: Safe â€” never low-level (forced off for all edit listeners)
items.define: `listenEdit('editInput', function(id, value, meta) ... return value end)`
items.fires: on the submitted user text, before it is stored as the user message

items.title: Hook: `editOutput` (mode `editOutput`)
items.name: editOutput
items.summary: `editOutput` transforms the model reply text before it becomes the stored assistant message.
items.permission_tier: Safe â€” never low-level (forced off for all edit listeners)
items.define: `listenEdit('editOutput', function(id, value, meta) ... return value end)`
items.fires: on the model reply text, before/while it is stored as the assistant message

items.title: Hook: `editRequest` (mode `editRequest`)
items.name: editRequest
items.summary: `editRequest` lets you inspect or rewrite the final outgoing message array without touching stored chat.
items.permission_tier: Safe â€” never low-level (forced off for all edit listeners)
items.define: `listenEdit('editRequest', function(id, value, meta) ... return value end)`
items.fires: after the outgoing prompt has been fully assembled (memory + lorebook), before it is sent to the model

items.title: Hook: `onButtonClick` (mode `onButtonClick`)
items.name: onButtonClick
items.summary: `onButtonClick` is the handler for buttons embedded in displayed messages or background HTML.
items.permission_tier: Safe (plus low-level if the trigger has `lowLevelAccess`)
items.fires: when a rendered chat button carrying `risu-btn="payload"` is clicked

items.title: Hook: `onInput` (mode `input`)
items.name: onInput
items.summary: `onInput` runs at the very start of a send, the moment the user submits text.
items.permission_tier: Safe (plus low-level if the character has `lowLevelAccess`)
items.fires: when the user submits input, before that text is stored as the user message

items.title: Hook: `onOutput` (mode `output`)
items.name: onOutput
items.summary: `onOutput` runs once the assistant reply is present in the chat.
items.permission_tier: Safe (plus low-level if the character has `lowLevelAccess`)
items.fires: after the model reply has been added to the chat

items.title: Hook: `onStart` (mode `start`)
items.name: onStart
items.summary: `onStart` runs once per send, at the start of request assembly. By this point the latest user message has already been stored in the chat, but Risu has not yet assembled the prompt (description, persona, lorebooks, history, â€¦) or sent anything to the model. Mutating chat here therefore changes the upcoming request.
items.permission_tier: Safe (plus low-level if the character has `lowLevelAccess`)
items.fires: after the user message is stored, before the chat history is built into the model request
